surface.name: Working with Cohere's API and SDK, Python Chat sample
surface.selection_basis: The documentation home exposes API reference as a primary start surface. Its general About page states that the Cohere SDK is the primary access method and presents installation plus hello-world Chat snippets. Python is the first language tab; the other SDKs are equivalent language branches.

alternatives_considered.name: TypeScript SDK sample
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer language tab with the same Chat-response boundary; Python appears first.

alternatives_considered.name: OpenAI Compatibility API quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is for developers migrating an OpenAI-SDK application, not the native SDK route Cohere calls primary.

alternatives_considered.name: Build an Onboarding Assistant
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a seven-part learning sequence, while the API-reference start page supplies a shorter native SDK hello-world path.

alternatives_considered.name: Playground
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The docs navigation exposes Playground, but the API reference says the SDK is the primary way to access models.

entry_point.developer_intent: Use Cohere's native Python SDK and automatically issued trial key to send and print a hello-world Chat response.
assumed_prior_state: The developer does not yet have a Cohere account or API key.
The developer can run Python and pip locally.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Get started with the primary Cohere SDK using its installation method and code snippet.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A Python program creates Cohere ClientV2 with a trial API key, sends hello world to a Command model through Chat, and prints the response.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The executed code reaches print(response) and emits a Cohere Chat response object.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The general API and SDK start page ends each language's initial Chat snippet by printing or logging the response.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The response is the terminal action of the native SDK hello-world snippet on the page Cohere describes as the primary access method. Other endpoints, compatibility APIs, tutorials, and production keys are later or specialized paths.

prerequisites.requirement: A Cohere account; account creation automatically issues a Trial API key.
Python and pip for the first/default SDK tab.
A Trial API key is sufficient for non-production experimentation and is free but rate-limited.

primary_path.action: Open Cohere Documentation and select API reference.
details: The home page presents Guides and concepts, API reference, release notes, and cookbooks.
primary_path.input: Intent to make a first Cohere API call.
primary_path.success_signal: Working with Cohere's API and SDK is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The documentation home does not itself label one universal guide before the API surface.

details: Every account begins as a personal account with trial-key access.
primary_path.input: Supported signup identity and acceptance of current account terms.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The official sources inspected do not enumerate sign-up fields, verification, or provider-specific transitions.

primary_path.action: Automatically create a Trial API key for the new account.
primary_path.output: Trial API key on the dashboard.
primary_path.success_signal: The key appears in the API Keys dashboard section.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Provisioning timing and recovery are not documented.

primary_path.action: Open API Keys in the dashboard and copy the Trial API key.
primary_path.success_signal: The key is available for the SDK client.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Trial keys are free but limited and not allowed for production or commercial use.

primary_path.action: Install or upgrade the Cohere Python SDK.
primary_path.input: Python environment, pip, and network access.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Package resolution, permissions, or environment errors are not decomposed.

primary_path.action: Create runnable Python code and import cohere.
primary_path.input: Installed SDK and writable Python environment.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An import error indicates installation or environment mismatch.

primary_path.action: Instantiate cohere.ClientV2 with the copied API key.
details: The official snippet passes the API key directly to the client.
primary_path.input: Cohere SDK and Trial API key.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid, missing, revoked, or mis-copied keys prevent authenticated requests.

primary_path.action: Add the Chat request from the Python example.
details: Use the documented Command model and one user message with content hello world!.
primary_path.success_signal: The request includes model and messages.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Model-name availability can change independently of the SDK.

primary_path.action: Execute the program so co.chat sends the request.
primary_path.input: Runnable program, network access, and trial capacity.
primary_path.success_signal: The API returns a response instead of an exception.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Authentication, model access, network, monthly limits, or per-minute limits can fail the call.

primary_path.action: Print and inspect the returned response.
primary_path.success_signal: print(response) emits the model response, completing the sample.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No response or an exception means the first success was not reached.

branches.condition: The developer chooses TypeScript, Java, or Go.
branches.path: Use the language-specific install command, client construction, Chat call, and console/log output shown on the same page.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The tooling and syntax change; the final Chat response remains the same boundary.

branches.condition: The developer needs production or commercial use.
branches.path: An Owner opens Billing and Usage, starts Get Your Production key, and completes the Go to Production workflow.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds owner permission, application, billing, and paid-usage gates; it is not required for the trial hello-world call.

branches.condition: The developer already uses the OpenAI SDK.
branches.path: Follow the Compatibility API quickstart, configure Cohere's compatibility base URL and key, then send a Chat Completions request.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Reuses the OpenAI client instead of Cohere's native SDK.

friction_gates.description: A Cohere account is required before the dashboard issues a trial key.
The SDK requires a copied API key; the native sample passes it directly into the client.
The primary Python route requires a local SDK install.
Trial API calls are free but rate-limited, limited to experimentation, and not permitted for production or commercial use.
The API call waits on the hosted model response; no vendor duration is stated.

sources.title: Cohere Documentation
evidence_supported: API reference, guide, cookbook, and endpoint alternatives

sources.title: Working with Cohere's API and SDK
evidence_supported: Complete install, client, Chat, and print sequence

sources.title: Cohere Pricing
sections_used: How do I get a Trial API key?
How do I get a Production API key?
What's the difference between a Trial API key and Production API key?
Are there any account limitations upon signup?

sources.title: Different Types of API Keys and Rate Limits
Using Cohere models via the OpenAI SDK
evidence_supported: OpenAI SDK configuration and response boundary

uncertainties.question: What exact screens, fields, terms, and verification transitions appear during Cohere account creation?
uncertainties.impact: The live account journey cannot be decomposed to screen level.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official docs and pricing page say to create an account and describe the resulting trial key, but do not document registration steps.

uncertainties.question: What exact text should the native Python hello-world sample return?
uncertainties.impact: Verification can assert a valid Chat response but not a fixed sentence.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The general native SDK page prints the response but does not publish a deterministic sample output, and model output can vary.
